# Lombok

[← Back to README](../README.md)

---

**Lombok** is an annotation processor that generates boilerplate Java code at compile time — getters, setters, constructors, builders, equals/hashCode, and more. The generated bytecode is identical to hand-written code with zero runtime overhead.

---

## Maven Dependency

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
    <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
    <optional>true</optional>   <!-- compile-only; not included in final JAR -->
</dependency>
```

IDE plugin required: **Lombok plugin** for IntelliJ IDEA or VS Code Java extension.

---

## Core Annotations

### `@Getter` / `@Setter`

```java
@Getter
@Setter
public class Product {
    private Long id;
    private String name;
    private BigDecimal price;
}

// Generated:
// getId(), setId(Long)
// getName(), setName(String)
// getPrice(), setPrice(BigDecimal)
```

Field-level control:

```java
public class User {
    @Getter @Setter private String username;
    @Getter private String email;           // no setter
    @Getter(AccessLevel.PRIVATE) private String passwordHash;  // private getter only
}
```

### `@ToString`

```java
@ToString(exclude = "passwordHash")
public class User {
    private String username;
    private String email;
    private String passwordHash;
}
// toString() → User(username=alice, email=alice@example.com)
```

### `@EqualsAndHashCode`

```java
@EqualsAndHashCode(of = {"id"})   // only use id for equality
public class Order {
    private UUID id;
    private String customerId;
    private List<OrderLine> lines;
}
```

### `@NoArgsConstructor` / `@RequiredArgsConstructor` / `@AllArgsConstructor`

```java
@NoArgsConstructor                  // public Order() {}
@RequiredArgsConstructor            // constructor for final + @NonNull fields
@AllArgsConstructor                 // constructor for all fields
public class Order {
    private UUID id;
    @NonNull private String customerId;  // included in @RequiredArgsConstructor
    private OrderStatus status;
}
```

---

## `@Data` — The Kitchen Sink

`@Data` = `@Getter` + `@Setter` + `@RequiredArgsConstructor` + `@ToString` + `@EqualsAndHashCode`

```java
@Data
public class Address {
    private String street;
    private String city;
    private String country;
    private String postalCode;
}
```

**Avoid `@Data` on JPA entities** — the generated `equals`/`hashCode` uses all fields including mutable ones, which breaks Hibernate's identity tracking. Use explicit `@Id`-based equals instead.

---

## `@Builder`

```java
@Builder
@Getter
public class CreateOrderCommand {
    private final String customerId;
    private final List<OrderLine> lines;
    private final String promoCode;

    @Builder.Default
    private final Instant createdAt = Instant.now();  // default value in builder
}

// Usage
CreateOrderCommand cmd = CreateOrderCommand.builder()
    .customerId("CUST-123")
    .lines(List.of(new OrderLine("PROD-1", 2)))
    .promoCode("SAVE10")
    .build();
```

### `@Builder` with Inheritance

```java
@Getter
@SuperBuilder   // use @SuperBuilder for inheritance
public class BaseEntity {
    private UUID id;
    private Instant createdAt;
}

@Getter
@SuperBuilder
public class Order extends BaseEntity {
    private String customerId;
    private BigDecimal total;
}

Order order = Order.builder()
    .id(UUID.randomUUID())
    .createdAt(Instant.now())
    .customerId("CUST-1")
    .total(new BigDecimal("99.99"))
    .build();
```

---

## `@Value` — Immutable Classes

`@Value` = `@Getter` + `@AllArgsConstructor` + `@ToString` + `@EqualsAndHashCode` + all fields `private final`

```java
@Value
public class Money {
    BigDecimal amount;
    String currency;
}

// Usage
Money price = new Money(new BigDecimal("9.99"), "USD");
// price.setAmount(...) → COMPILE ERROR — no setters generated
```

---

## `@Slf4j` — Logging

```java
@Slf4j
@Service
public class OrderService {

    public Order placeOrder(PlaceOrderCommand cmd) {
        log.info("Placing order for customer {}", cmd.getCustomerId());
        log.debug("Order lines: {}", cmd.getLines());
        // ...
    }
}

// Generated:
// private static final org.slf4j.Logger log =
//     org.slf4j.LoggerFactory.getLogger(OrderService.class);
```

Other logging variants: `@Log`, `@Log4j2`, `@CommonsLog`.

---

## `@NonNull`

```java
public class OrderService {

    public Order findById(@NonNull UUID id) {
        // Generated: if (id == null) throw new NullPointerException("id is marked @NonNull...")
        return repo.findById(id).orElseThrow();
    }
}
```

---

## `@Cleanup` — Auto-Close Resources

```java
public void processFile(String path) throws IOException {
    @Cleanup InputStream is = new FileInputStream(path);
    // is.close() called automatically — even on exception
    // equivalent to try-with-resources
}
```

---

## `@SneakyThrows` — Unchecked Checked Exceptions

```java
@SneakyThrows(IOException.class)   // rethrows as unchecked — use sparingly
public String readFile(Path path) {
    return Files.readString(path);
}
```

---

## `@Locked` / `@Synchronized`

```java
@Getter
public class Counter {

    private int count;

    @Synchronized   // locks on a private object, not 'this'
    public void increment() {
        count++;
    }
}
```

---

## Lombok with Records

Records (Java 16+) replace much of what Lombok does for immutable data:

```java
// Prefer records for pure data carriers
public record Money(BigDecimal amount, String currency) {}

// Use Lombok @Builder when you need a builder on a record
@Builder
public record CreateOrderCommand(
    String customerId,
    List<OrderLine> lines,
    String promoCode
) {}
```

---

## Lombok and JPA — What to Avoid

```java
// BAD — @Data on JPA entity
@Data           // generates equals/hashCode using all fields
@Entity
public class Order { ... }  // breaks Hibernate — use explicit equals by @Id

// GOOD
@Getter
@Setter
@NoArgsConstructor          // JPA requires no-arg constructor
@EqualsAndHashCode(of = "id")
@Entity
public class Order {
    @Id @GeneratedValue
    private UUID id;
    // ...
}
```

Also avoid `@ToString` on entities with lazy relationships — it can trigger unexpected queries.

---

## Lombok Summary

| Annotation | Generates |
|------------|-----------|
| `@Getter` / `@Setter` | Getters and/or setters |
| `@ToString` | `toString()` |
| `@EqualsAndHashCode` | `equals()` + `hashCode()` |
| `@NoArgsConstructor` | No-arg constructor |
| `@RequiredArgsConstructor` | Constructor for `final` + `@NonNull` fields |
| `@AllArgsConstructor` | Constructor for every field |
| `@Data` | All of the above combined (mutable) |
| `@Value` | All of the above (immutable — all `final`) |
| `@Builder` | Fluent builder + `build()` |
| `@SuperBuilder` | Builder for class hierarchies |
| `@Slf4j` | `private static final Logger log = ...` |
| `@NonNull` | Null check at top of method / constructor |
| `@Cleanup` | Auto-close resources (like try-with-resources) |
| `@SneakyThrows` | Rethrow checked exceptions as unchecked |

---
